title: Portfolio Site Refresh Goes Live
short_description: The public portfolio has been refreshed with a faster Next.js frontend, CMS-driven content, and a new updates section for news and announcements.
content:
Visitors can now browse projects, case studies, insights, and the latest homepage updates from a single responsive experience.

This release improves performance, accessibility, and makes it easier to publish news without redeploying the site.

title: CMS Admin Now Supports Posters and News
short_description: Editors can create, publish, and feature posters directly from the Laravel admin panel. Published items appear automatically on the homepage.
content:
The new Posters / News module supports categories such as announcements, blogs, entertainment, and general updates.

Draft content stays hidden until it is published, and featured posts are highlighted in the Latest Updates section.

title: Why Integration Design Belongs With System Analysis
short_description: Strong system analysis should shape API contracts, data ownership, and service boundaries before development accelerates.
content:
When integration decisions are deferred, teams pay for them later in rework, fragile interfaces, and unclear accountability.

A practical approach is to pair process modeling with interface design early, then validate assumptions through thin vertical slices.

title: Open for Enterprise Consulting Engagements
short_description: Accepting new system analysis, architecture review, and digital transformation engagements for Q3 2026.
content: If you are modernizing legacy platforms, standardizing APIs, or preparing for a major release, reach out through the contact page to discuss scope and timelines.

title: Architecture Sketch Session Highlights
short_description: A quick look at a whiteboard session mapping service flows, actors, and integration touchpoints for a public-sector platform.
content:
These sessions help stakeholders align on scope before detailed design begins. The visual format makes dependencies and hand-offs easier to discuss.

Even a one-hour sketch can surface risks that spreadsheets and slide decks often hide.
